sections.title: On the IVSH:
path: Opinion of the IVSH on the consultation "Digital Fairness Act"

paragraphs.text: IVSH Industrial association of cutting and household goods

section_path: Opinion of the IVSH on the consultation "Digital Fairness Act"
paragraphs.text: We strongly welcome the call for evidence and the current consultation on the Digital Fairness Act (DFA). In particular, in the context of the 2024 fitness check on digital fairness, which identified consumer protection gaps online, we consider this initiative to be strongly supported. In our view, the role and failures of large online platforms in trade in goods and in consumer products must be addressed much more in the DFA, particularly with regard to goods, advertising and sales.

section_path: Opinion of the IVSH on the consultation "Digital Fairness Act"
paragraphs.text: This is the only way for the DFA to respond effectively to the current challenges and unfair practices of an increasingly digitalised market.

section_path: Opinion of the IVSH on the consultation "Digital Fairness Act"
paragraphs.text: In our opinion, the DFA should put enforcement before new rules: Consumer protection and rights only work if they are effectively enforced against all market actors, in particular platforms and third country suppliers. At the same time, requirements must remain proportionate in order not to put serious European SMEs at an additional disadvantage.

section_path: Opinion of the IVSH on the consultation "Digital Fairness Act"
paragraphs.text: A problem which, in our view, is still not sufficiently addressed: Role of online platforms & two-tier company in enforcement

section_path: Opinion of the IVSH on the consultation "Digital Fairness Act"
paragraphs.text: In the Single Market, there is an increasing threat of a two-tier digital company: Responsible European producers/traders meet high standards, while suppliers on marketplaces such as Temu or Shein systematically meet them

section_path: Opinion of the IVSH on the consultation "Digital Fairness Act"
paragraphs.text: IVSH position DFA Page 1 of 3 September 2025 Industry association Schneid- und Hauswaren e.V. Neuenhofer Straße 24 D-42657 Solingen

section_path: Opinion of the IVSH on the consultation "Digital Fairness Act"
paragraphs.text: undermined – from labelling and product safety to traceability. In many cases, actual producers are inconceivable; in doing so, they avoid any responsibility. This was unfortunately also reflected in our IVSH-Temu Test Purchase Action: various serious

section_path: Opinion of the IVSH on the consultation "Digital Fairness Act"
paragraphs.text: Infringements have been able to documented instruments.

section_path: Opinion of the IVSH on the consultation "Digital Fairness Act"
paragraphs.text: For example, the EU has classified Temu as a VLOP under the DSA. It is now crucial to consistently enforce DSA obligations. If platforms or their providers continue to disregard EU law and do not take effective remedies, market access bans must be possible up to (temporary) shutdowns. Anything else undermines the credibility of the legal framework. Especially since such platforms operate with sometimes very worrying customer-addressing, customer loyalty and Co. tools (see below). These issues should be addressed systematically and consistently in the DFA. Especially as there is an asymmetry not only between platforms and customers, but also as medium-sized EU suppliers, which are increasingly pushed out of the market by unjustified and sometimes borderline practices. Here too, there is a need for more digital fairness in the market.

section_path: Opinion of the IVSH on the consultation "Digital Fairness Act"
What the DFA should do/take into account
paragraphs.text: Anchoring DSA+ enforcement: Clear escalation levels (regulatory requirements, sensitive sanctions, traffic/location restrictions until temporary shutdown) in case of systematic breaches of EU law.

section_path: Opinion of the IVSH on the consultation "Digital Fairness Act"
What the DFA should do/take into account
paragraphs.text: Raise platform responsibility: Where sellers are not clear identifiable, the platform must act as a 'economic actor' for security, traceability and access to authorities ('no trader, platform liable').

section_path: Opinion of the IVSH on the consultation "Digital Fairness Act"
What the DFA should do/take into account
paragraphs.text: Traceability mandatory: EU-wide 'Know Your Business Customer' obligations, verified trader details, mandatory take-down

section_path: Opinion of the IVSH on the consultation "Digital Fairness Act"
What the DFA should do/take into account
paragraphs.text: Processes in the event of non-compliance, e.g. in the context of false product or

section_path: Opinion of the IVSH on the consultation "Digital Fairness Act"
What the DFA should do/take into account
paragraphs.text: origin designations ('made in Germany'/'made in Solingen', etc.)

section_path: Opinion of the IVSH on the consultation "Digital Fairness Act"
What the DFA should do/take into account
paragraphs.text: Dark patterns; Influencer advertising; 'addictive design', unfair Customisation: Clear bans on manipulative designs, transparent Labelling from Advertising/recommendations; by default low-tampering defaults – with special protection of juvenile users.

section_path: Opinion of the IVSH on the consultation "Digital Fairness Act"
What the DFA should do/take into account
paragraphs.text: Proportionality & SME protection: Risk and evidence-based requirements; proportionate reporting cycles; No trickle down of disproportionate obligations on SMEs and mid-caps (clear value-chain cut-off).

section_path: Opinion of the IVSH on the consultation "Digital Fairness Act"
What the DFA should do/take into account
paragraphs.text: Strengthening cross-border enforcement: Swiftly implement EU-wide mix of measures along the lines of the proposals of the German E-Commerce Action Plan of BMWE and Customs Reform (DIHK) – customs, small consignments, data access, coordinated action.

section_path: Opinion of the IVSH on the consultation "Digital Fairness Act"
Target image of the DFA (incl.)
paragraphs.text: A DFA that unites Level Playing Field and Effective Protection: strong, noticeable enforcement against substandard platform ecosystems, combined with practical, proportional obligations for compliant European companies. This will protect consumers and ensure competitiveness.

section_path: Opinion of the IVSH on the consultation "Digital Fairness Act"
On the IVSH:
paragraphs.text: The Schneid- und Hauswaren Industry Association represents almost all companies in the sector (representing tens of thousands of jobs and billions of euros of turnover) as the general and only representative of the German cutting and household goods industry, and sees itself as a modern, constructive and efficient platform for pooling and focusing the interests, themes and potentials of our sector.
